title: The RealReal CFO Touts Supply-Led Growth, AI Efficiencies as GMV Accelerates at Conference
text: The RealReal's CFO discussed supply-led growth and AI efficiency gains at an investor conference in March 2026. The company is focusing on unlocking additional supply through retail store expansion in new markets (San Francisco, Boston announced for 2026) and partnerships with stylists, closet organizers, and real estate agents. The Real Partners program enables referral-based consignment with commission incentives, creating a scalable supply channel. AI-driven efficiency improvements through Athena system continue to drive cost reductions in authentication and processing. The CFO highlighted that margins are expanding significantly due to fixed cost leverage from revenue growth combined with variable cost reductions from automation. Trailing twelve-month active buyers grew 10% in Q4 2025, with 43% of new consigners converting from existing buyers. This flywheel dynamic indicates strong network effects and retention. Management projected continued momentum into 2026 with supply-led initiatives supporting GMV growth of 12%-15% and margin expansion of nearly 200 basis points versus 2025. The company is well-positioned to benefit from secular tailwinds in luxury resale market growth and premium consumer spending trends.
